title: pilosella callimorphoides subsp. pseudoschultesiiformis
intro: Pilosella callimorphoides subsp. pseudoschultesiiformis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a type of hawkweed. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Pilosella callimorphoides subsp. pseudoschultesiiformis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although its preparation and dosage should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
